content:
Entire class is forced to speak in only one-syllable words for a debate.

✅ Why This Works:
• Ties narrative perspective to decision-making.
• Ensures that even chaotic choices are rooted in structured concepts.
• Encourages participation by making every class unpredictable.

📌 Version 4.9: Final Questions Before Lock-In

1. Should students be able to switch POV narrators at the start of class, or should the instructor select them?

✔ Instructor rotates through narrators, but students can “spend” XP to override the selection.

2. Should failing a Redemption Path provide an alternate “Penalty Path” before full removal?

✔ Example: If a student fails their Redemption Path, they must submit a final “Confession of Failure” analyzing where they went wrong before being locked out of Mastery Exams.
context: From document: RPG Character System (Fully Integrated into Weekly Modules) — chunk 110 (chunk 110/555)
